topics.heading: Dockerfile Best Practices
points: **Base Image** — Use minimal images like `alpine` or `eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine`. Smaller image = faster pulls, smaller attack surface, less storage.
**Multi-Stage Builds** — Build stage compiles with Maven/Gradle; final stage copies only the JAR. `FROM maven AS build` then `FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine`.
**Layer Caching** — Order Dockerfile instructions from least to most frequently changing. Copy `pom.xml` and download dependencies before copying source code.
**Security** — Run as non-root user (`RUN adduser --system app` + `USER app`). Scan images with Trivy or Snyk. Use `.dockerignore` to exclude sensitive files.
**HEALTHCHECK** — `HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/actuator/health || exit 1`. Enables Docker to detect unhealthy containers and restart them.

topics.heading: Docker Compose
points: **Service Definition** — Define services, networks, and volumes in `docker-compose.yml`. Each service maps to a container with its own config, ports, and environment.
**Dependencies** — `depends_on` controls startup order. Use `healthcheck` + `condition: service_healthy` to wait for actual readiness, not just container start.
**Networking** — Services on same network communicate by service name. Default bridge network created automatically. Custom networks for isolation.
**Volumes** — Named volumes for persistent data (databases). Bind mounts for development (live code reload). `volumes:` section for reusable volume definitions.
**Environment Variables** — `.env` file for defaults, `environment:` in compose file, or `env_file:` for per-service config. Override with `docker compose --env-file`.

topics.heading: GitHub Actions
points: **Workflow Structure** — YAML in `.github/workflows/`. Triggers: `push`, `pull_request`, `schedule`, `workflow_dispatch`. Jobs run on runners (ubuntu-latest, self-hosted).
**Steps & Actions** — Steps execute commands or reuse actions. `uses: actions/checkout@v4`, `uses: actions/setup-java@v4`. `run:` for shell commands.
**Caching** — Cache Maven/Gradle dependencies: `actions/cache@v4` with `~/.m2` or `~/.gradle/caches`. Speeds up builds significantly.
**Secrets & Environment** — Store secrets in repo/org settings. Access via `${{ secrets.DOCKER_PASSWORD }}`. Environment protection rules for production deployments.
**Matrix Builds** — Test across multiple versions: `strategy: matrix: java: [17, 21]`. Runs parallel jobs for each combination.

topics.heading: Deployment Strategies
points: **Blue-Green Deployment** — Two identical environments. Route traffic from blue (current) to green (new) after validation. Instant rollback by switching back.
**Canary Deployment** — Route small percentage of traffic (5-10%) to new version. Monitor error rates and latency. Gradually increase if healthy.
**Rolling Deployment** — Replace instances one at a time. No downtime. Kubernetes default strategy. `maxSurge` and `maxUnavailable` control rollout speed.
**Feature Flags** — Toggle features without deployment. LaunchDarkly, Unleash, or custom implementation. Decouple deployment from release. A/B testing support.
**Rollback Strategy** — Automated rollback on health check failure. Keep previous version artifacts. Database migrations must be backward-compatible for safe rollback.

topics.heading: Core Concepts
points: **Pod** — Smallest deployable unit. One or more containers sharing network and storage. Ephemeral by design; managed by controllers, not created directly.
**Deployment** — Manages ReplicaSets for declarative updates. Defines desired state (replicas, image, strategy). Supports rolling updates and rollbacks.
**Service** — Stable network endpoint for Pods. ClusterIP (internal), NodePort (external port on each node), LoadBalancer (cloud LB), ExternalName (DNS alias).
**Ingress** — HTTP/HTTPS routing to services. Host-based and path-based routing. TLS termination. Requires Ingress Controller (NGINX, Traefik, AWS ALB).
**Namespace** — Logical cluster isolation. `default`, `kube-system`, custom namespaces per team/environment. Resource quotas and network policies per namespace.

topics.heading: Config & Storage
points: **ConfigMap** — Store non-sensitive config as key-value pairs. Mount as environment variables or volume files. `kubectl create configmap --from-file`.
**Secret** — Base64-encoded sensitive data. Types: Opaque, docker-registry, TLS. Use Sealed Secrets or External Secrets Operator for encryption at rest.
**PersistentVolume (PV)** — Cluster storage resource. `PersistentVolumeClaim` (PVC) requests storage. StorageClass for dynamic provisioning (EBS, GCE PD, NFS).
**Resource Limits** — `requests` (guaranteed) and `limits` (maximum) for CPU and memory. Prevents noisy neighbors. OOMKilled if memory limit exceeded.
**Init Containers** — Run before app containers. Use for setup tasks: wait for dependencies, run migrations, download configs. Must complete successfully before app starts.

topics.heading: Scaling & Health
points: **HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler)** — Auto-scale pods based on CPU, memory, or custom metrics. `minReplicas: 2, maxReplicas: 10, targetCPUUtilizationPercentage: 70`.
**Liveness Probe** — Detects if container is stuck. Restarts container on failure. HTTP GET, TCP socket, or exec command. Configure `initialDelaySeconds` and `periodSeconds`.
**Readiness Probe** — Detects if container can serve traffic. Removes from Service endpoints when failing. Prevents routing to starting or unhealthy pods.
**Resource Quotas** — Limit total resources per namespace. `requests.cpu`, `limits.memory`, `count/pods`. Prevents one team from consuming entire cluster.
**Pod Disruption Budget (PDB)** — Guarantees minimum available pods during voluntary disruptions (node drain, upgrades). `minAvailable: 2` or `maxUnavailable: 1`.
